Definition: Improvement is linked to a documented initiative with comparable baseline/current data.
Required_Evidence: Initiative record + baseline/current data + scope consistency
Scoring_Implication: Full outcome consideration if evidence and DQ are sufficient

Definition: Improvement likely results from several factors, including at least one documented intervention.
Required_Evidence: Intervention evidence + explanation of other factors
Scoring_Implication: Partial outcome consideration; confidence note required
Example: Water use reduced after new controls and lower occupancy.

Definition: Performance improved because activity, production, occupancy, or service level decreased.
Scoring_Implication: Do not treat as true improvement unless normalized metrics also improve
Example: Electricity fell because a facility closed.

Definition: Improvement results from changing assessment boundary, outsourcing, excluding sites, or scope adjustments.
Scoring_Implication: Flag and adjust baseline; avoid direct improvement credit

Definition: No clear evidence explaining the trend.
Scoring_Implication: Trend may be reported but not scored as proven improvement
Example: Water decreased but no initiative or activity data.
